item: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.
notes: Both agents clearly reject the statement from opening to final turn. Agent A repeatedly argues that collective-interest language can justify abuse of weak citizens, censorship, and minority harms, while allowing narrow public-health or safety limits. Agent B makes the same rights-first argument in U.S. terms, with narrow exceptions for real and accountable harms. No visible flip or meaningful position shift is observable in the text.
